name: Lightstep (ServiceNow Cloud Observability)
what_it_does: Distributed tracing and observability platform (now part of ServiceNow as Cloud Observability) that analyzes OpenTelemetry traces at scale, with APIs for querying telemetry data and managing observability configuration.
use_cases: Querying trace data to identify performance regressions in distributed services
Programmatically managing alert conditions and SLO configurations
Integrating trace analysis into CI/CD pipelines for performance regression detection
Correlating deploy events with performance changes via the Change Intelligence API
Building custom observability dashboards from trace and metric data
not_for: Infrastructure-level metrics monitoring (use Prometheus or Datadog for host metrics)
Log aggregation as primary use case
Organizations not already using OpenTelemetry or distributed tracing
Small teams without distributed service architectures
best_when: You're using OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing in microservices and want deep trace analytics with Change Intelligence to correlate deployments with performance.
avoid_when: You don't have distributed services generating trace data, or you're already committed to Honeycomb or Datadog APM.

auth.notes: API keys are project-scoped. Created in the Lightstep console. No fine-grained scope control per key.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing based on span volume; requires sales engagement
pricing.notes: No public pricing or self-service trial. Enterprise contract required. Pricing based on spans ingested per month.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standard HTTP codes. Error messages are functional but not especially detailed. Documentation quality is lower than Honeycomb or Datadog.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent. Alert and SLO creation has idempotency via name-based deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Product has been rebranded to ServiceNow Cloud Observability — documentation may reference both names inconsistently
Project and organization IDs are distinct concepts — both required for most API calls
Span data is sampled — query results represent sampled data, not 100% of spans
API access patterns may evolve post-ServiceNow acquisition — verify API stability before long-term investment
Change Intelligence events require specific ingestion format — review documentation before integration

security_score_components.security_notes: Observability/APM platform (now ServiceNow Cloud Observability). API key auth. Trace data may contain sensitive request payloads. RBAC for data access.
